children.text: Documenting the days of District past
We are currently rebuilding our site. This section in particular requires some work to add additional content and photos, to existing and new articles.  Please revisit for new content.
If you have any content you would like to share, please send to:
mail@obpsfa.com

mainImage.caption: 2003-2004 squad

seo.description: Photos and stories of the District.

children.text: On Sunday the third of April the Orpington Blues played against Gloucester a in a thrilling match hoping to come out on top.
Kick off:
Straight away the blues were on the attack testing the Gloucester goalkeeper.
Near the end off the first half Nathan had a banging free kick that went just wide.
Second half
once again the blues were on the attack firing shots at the poor Gloucester keeper
Orpington were on the attack George had the ball at his feet and hit a wonderful strike beating the keeper,the boys went crazy leaping on top of the goal scorer George.
1-0 Orpington Blues!!!
the boys were still going on strong , Jessie made a great last ditch challenge.
noah had the ball at his feet and got fouled inside the area
PENALTY
Noah steeped up to take the penalty and missed , disaster struck
the opponents were on the attack and goal
the life from the players had drained,once again noah had the ball at his feet and worked his way into the area and was fouled.
penalty? but no it was not a penalty it was a free kick right on the edge of the area
full time,the game finished 1-1
After the game we came home for lunch and went to the Aquasplash pool and Noah, James and Max had fun drowning each other.
WE HAD A SICK DAY 2GETHER

seo.description: On Sunday the third of April the Orpington Blues played against Gloucester a in a thrilling match hoping to come out on top.

children.text: Yesterday we went to the park and played hide and seek. After the park we went to the hotel swimming poo; and played frisbee. Before that we had a football match at 1015 against St Pauls u12s, but we don’t want to talk about it!

seo.description: Yesterday we went to the park and played hide and seek. After the park we went to the hotel swimming poo; and played frisbee.
Photos of the the 2014-2015 squads, taken during their trip to Jersey.

node.title: Final Day Match Report Blue And Red

children.text: Just a quick final day update, as it is such a busy day, what with packing and playing and shopping. Getting a match report written by the boys is tough to squeeze in.
Blues and Reds were drawn against both Gloucester A and B Squads. Blues kicking off first then the Reds. Which really gives the boys the chance to support each other.
Blues:

After yesterdays win the spirits were good in the camp and the boys were really looking forward to the last game here in Jersey. Gloucester are a credit to their district and play the game in a great spirit. The blues started off with a flyer. Josh taking the ball on the right and driving towards the by line then unleashing an absolute rocket across the keeper that whacked into the back of the net. From then on in the first half the boys didn’t look back and after a period of pressure Jack connected with a great shot from outside the area that took a deflection and went past the keeper. Jack was quick to claim it. The dubious goals committee are looking at video reply!
Taking a 2-0 lead into the second half, Gloucester really played well and put everyone under pressure but Adam, Alex, Dan stayed strong in the second half and kept a clean sheet for a great victory.
Reds:
As with the Blues after a stunning win against Plymouth yesterday spirits were sky high in the team and really looking forward to today’s challenge against our friends from Gloucester.

In a tight opening 15 minutes both sides attacked and defended with real pace, belying the fact there was some tired bodies out there. After a neat interchange in midfield the ball was moved out to Alfie on the left, who cut inside, Ronaldo like, and bent a shot across the keeper and into the net. Cue the celebration’s from Reds and Blues. The game continued to ebb and flow through the rest of the first half.

Gloucester started the second half brightly and really put the Reds under pressure. Pavel was really in the game and pulled off a Gordon Bank’s like save. Quickly down to his right, a save of outstanding quality. The reds held firm, the defence making sure the ball was cleared promptly and Alfie and Joe trying their best to hold the ball up when it got to them. As the game wore on the Reds Centre midfield started to take control and the flanks really came into it and pressure was relieved. Kai found himself 20 yards out form goal with the ball set it on his right foot, cut it back and blasted a left foot shot over the keepers head and off the underside of the bar and into the net. Two goals in two games for the midfield dynamo. The reds saw the game out for another fine victory, a true team performance!
Well done to both squads for finishing strong in the festival, some very fit strong boys.
Many thanks for all the support, both here and from back home. Much appreciated by all!

seo.description: Just a quick final day update, as it is such a busy day, what with packing and playing and shopping. Getting a match report written by the boys is tough to squeeze in.

children.text: On Sunday 5th April the Jersey Festival of 2015 got under way. The matches were played at the F.B playing fields which was the best pitches we ever played on as a team. Everyone gave 100% and tried their very best.

Pawel pulled off some acrobatic saves and came off his line a numerous amount of times to stop their striker scoring.
Joseph Furzer only played 4 games and in all of them he was brilliant. Taking the ball away from the opponents feet several times with an inch perfect slide tackle.

Ethan won every header and made about 100 perfect tackles.
That was the same for James who also put in some lovely passes through for the wingers to run onto.
Kai played most of his games in CDM role which he was brilliant in, he also scored a few stunning goals too.

Josh played in many positions such as : LM,GK,ST,CM,CB and LB, In all these positions he was excellent and did what he was asked to do.
Daniel played great the whole tournament getting loads of assists game after game, he also got himself a good goal against St Albans B.
Georgie was great as well, he beat the left back loads of times when he played RM and when he moved to RB no one got passed him.
Matthew was amazing yet again, he beat the RB like Georgie and put in some perfect crosses, he scored two breathtaking headers to sum up his time at Jersey.
Joseph Cotton was excellent upfront, he held up the ball well and scored many great goals.

Every afternoon after we played football, Mike took us somewhere fun. We went to 3 beaches, Aqua Splash, the zoo, the pool at the hotel and on the last day we went shopping. At one of the beaches there was loads of rock pools to climb on. Pawel could climb everything so we called him Spider-Man. At the two other beaches we marked out little pitches with the cricket stumps and we also done dizzy penalties. Aqua Splash was awesome as well, there was a wave pool, flumes and a diving board. Most of us went on everything, it was the best!

My favourite activity was going to the zoo, we saw loads of rare animals such as poisonous frogs, bears, Orangutans, gorillas, bats, snakes, komodo dragons, chameleons and loads of different species of monkeys. My favourite animal was the Orangutangs.
I can honestly say this has been one of the best experiences I’ve ever had, through this experience I’m sure we’ll all stay friends for ever and ever.
I want to say a big thank you to everyone at Orpington District for making this trip possible for us boys.

seo.authorDisplay: Alfie O’Neill, Captain of the Red Squad
